summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the OAuth client registration endpoint accepts cross-origin POST requests without CSRF token validation, allowing attacker-controlled websites to register OAuth clients in victim's context.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a cross-origin POST request from an attacker-controlled page can successfully register an OAuth client on the vulnerable server without CSRF token validation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the target MCP server with OAuth handlers, then the attacker page triggered a cross-origin POST to /oauth/register, and the server accepted the request and registered a new OAuth client, confirming the CSRF vulnerability.
verification_method: The reproduction script checks whether the OAuth client registration endpoint accepted the cross-origin POST request and created a client entry. A successful registration proves CSRF protection is missing.
evidence_produced_by_run: The target server accepted cross-origin POST request to /oauth/register
A new OAuth client was registered with attacker-controlled metadata
No CSRF token validation or origin check was performed
The CORS middleware allowed the cross-origin request from attacker origin

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out version 1.25.1, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
